2072170901714465132.headline: Ecuador Briefing
2072170901714465132.body: 𝗗𝗶𝗱𝗻’𝘁 𝗙𝗜𝗙𝗔 𝘀𝗮𝘆 𝗶𝘁 𝗵𝗮𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗵𝘆𝗱𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗯𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝘀? In announcing the delay at the Azteca, FIFA said that as a result the controversial cooling breaks would not happen during Mexico vs Ecuador. But the referee stopped play with around 23 minutes gone. Players walked to their sidelines in customary fashion, and the partisan Mexican crowd jeered briefly before singing “Mexico lindo” to pass the time. On FIFA’s website, the line that “No hydration breaks will take place during this match” had been removed from its statement. Mexico 2-0 Ecuador Briefing: bit.ly/4wECxHB Link Mexico defeat Ecuador for first World Cup knockout win since 1986, how far can Azteca magic take... 𝗗𝗶𝗱𝗻’𝘁 𝗙𝗜𝗙𝗔 𝘀𝗮𝘆 𝗶𝘁 𝗵𝗮𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗵𝘆𝗱𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗯𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝘀?

In announcing the delay at the Azteca, FIFA said that as a result the controversial cooling breaks would not happen during Mexico vs Ecuador.

But the referee stopped play with around 23 minutes gone. Players walked to their sidelines in customary fashion, and the partisan Mexican crowd jeered briefly before singing “Mexico lindo” to pass the time.

On FIFA’s website, the line that “No hydration breaks will take place during this match” had been removed from its statement.

Mexico 2-0 Ecuador Briefing: bit.ly/4wECxHB

2072169735819595801.headline: World Cup
2072169735819595801.body: Mexico beat Ecuador to win their first men's World Cup knockout stage match since 1986. Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez — the two scorers in Mexico's 2-0 victory in their World Cup opener against South Africa — earned their country a place in the last 16, where they will face either England or DR Congo in Mexico City on July 5. From the high of their win over Germany, Ecuador, who finished the game with 10 men following Piero Hincapie's red card for covering his mouth in a situation of confrontation, come back down to earth at a raucous Estadio Azteca.
2072169735819595801.raw_text:
Mexico beat Ecuador to win their first men's World Cup knockout stage match since 1986.

Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez — the two scorers in Mexico's 2-0 victory in their World Cup opener against South Africa — earned their country a place in the last 16, where they will face either England or DR Congo in Mexico City on July 5.

From the high of their win over Germany, Ecuador, who finished the game with 10 men following Piero Hincapie's red card for covering his mouth in a situation of confrontation, come back down to earth at a raucous Estadio Azteca.

2072169219983135208.headline: Piero Hincapie

2072238416251519019.headline: Sebastian Beccacece
2072238416251519019.body: Sebastian Beccacece announced his intention to step down as Ecuador head coach after his side exited the World Cup on Tuesday with defeat to Mexico. Ecuador were eliminated at the last-32 stage following a 2-0 loss to the co-hosts in Mexico City. Beccacece told reporters after Wednesday’s defeat that he would not be continuing in the role, explaining that although his side did not achieve what he had hoped, he was proud of “what we built”. More from AliRampling 🔗 nyti.ms/4b0mQlo
2072238416251519019.raw_text:
Sebastian Beccacece announced his intention to step down as Ecuador head coach after his side exited the World Cup on Tuesday with defeat to Mexico.

Ecuador were eliminated at the last-32 stage following a 2-0 loss to the co-hosts in Mexico City.

Beccacece told reporters after Wednesday’s defeat that he would not be continuing in the role, explaining that although his side did not achieve what he had hoped, he was proud of “what we built”.

More from @AliRampling

2072281940254658644.headline: Wayne Rooney
2072281940254658644.body: It's been 20 years since Wayne Rooney was shown a red card for stamping on Ricardo Carvalho. Of all the places to stamp as well... Rooney, who had suffered a metatarsal injury less than six weeks before the start of the 2006 World Cup, also shoved then Manchester United teammate Cristiano Ronaldo, who urged Argentine referee Horacio Elizondo to brandish a red card. Ronaldo later scored the winning spot kick as Portugal eventually won 3-1 on penalties: Frank Lampard, Steven Gerrard and Jamie Carragher failed to convert for England.
2072281940254658644.raw_text:
It's been 20 years since Wayne Rooney was shown a red card for stamping on Ricardo Carvalho.

Of all the places to stamp as well...

Rooney, who had suffered a metatarsal injury less than six weeks before the start of the 2006 World Cup, also shoved then Manchester United teammate Cristiano Ronaldo, who urged Argentine referee Horacio Elizondo to brandish a red card.

Ronaldo later scored the winning spot kick as Portugal eventually won 3-1 on penalties: Frank Lampard, Steven Gerrard and Jamie Carragher failed to convert for England.

2072307349847491051.body: Three people have died from suffocation during World Cup celebrations in Mexico. A 19-year-old woman, a 44-year-old man and a 48-year-old woman died after crowds took to the streets in Mexico City, the nation’s capital, following the country’s round-of-32 victory over Ecuador on Tuesday, according to the city’s public health authority. Three people have died from suffocation during World Cup celebrations in Mexico.

A 19-year-old woman, a 44-year-old man and a 48-year-old woman died after crowds took to the streets in Mexico City, the nation’s capital, following the country’s round-of-32 victory over Ecuador on Tuesday, according to the city’s public health authority.

The public health authority added that medical responders had performed CPR on all three of the victims but each had died from asphyxiation and been identified by their families.

2072309060884410382.headline: World Cup
2072309060884410382.body: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has also fallen foul of Germany’s World Cup elimination by Paraguay, after stumbling into a social media debacle following a communications blunder. In the minutes after the Germans were eliminated on penalties for the first time in their World Cup history, a post from Merz’s official X account was published, reading: “Even if elimination hurts: what a game. With your commitment and team spirit at this World Cup,” the post continued, addressing the beaten players, “you have thrilled our country. We are proud of you.” Germans were not thrilled by their team’s World Cup performances. They were certainly not proud of them, either, and were quick to respond to Merz’s post. Among the most popular responses were users accusing Chancellor Merz of being “detached” or, as is common among European leaders, using football to communicate with voters. According to the Chancellor’s office, Merz’s post was a due to a communications error resulting in the incorrect social media post had been sent. ✍️ SebSB 🔗 nyti.ms/3QUplih
2072309060884410382.raw_text:
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has also fallen foul of Germany’s World Cup elimination by Paraguay, after stumbling into a social media debacle following a communications blunder.

In the minutes after the Germans were eliminated on penalties for the first time in their World Cup history, a post from Merz’s official X account was published, reading: “Even if elimination hurts: what a game. With your commitment and team spirit at this World Cup,” the post continued, addressing the beaten players, “you have thrilled our country. We are proud of you.”

Germans were not thrilled by their team’s World Cup performances. They were certainly not proud of them, either, and were quick to respond to Merz’s post. Among the most popular responses were users accusing Chancellor Merz of being “detached” or, as is common among European leaders, using football to communicate with voters.

According to the Chancellor’s office, Merz’s post was a due to a communications error resulting in the incorrect social media post had been sent.

✍️ @SebSB 

🔗 nyti.ms/3QUplih

2072313276386467944.headline: Joan Laporta
2072313276386467944.body: 🚨 Joan Laporta: “We have a lot of respect for Atlético Madrid.. The tweets were before Julián spoke about his dream of playing for a big club, but he never mentioned Barcelona at any point; we did NOT force him into this”. “The player was on our radar even before he moved to Manchester City. What we did was a club-to-club offer. I see NO logic in the actions they're taking now”. “This isn't the first time we've done a deal with Atlético, but perhaps this one is more sensitive; we're putting in a great effort, and there are those who want to stir up controversy”.
2072313276386467944.raw_text:
🚨 Joan Laporta: “We have a lot of respect for Atlético Madrid.. The tweets were before Julián spoke about his dream of playing for a big club, but he never mentioned Barcelona at any point; we did NOT force him into this”.

“The player was on our radar even before he moved to Manchester City. What we did was a club-to-club offer. I see NO logic in the actions they're taking now”.

“This isn't the first time we've done a deal with Atlético, but perhaps this one is more sensitive; we're putting in a great effort, and there are those who want to stir up controversy”.

2072312938866655441.headline: Julián Álvarez
2072312938866655441.body: 🚨🔵🔴 Laporta on Julián Álvarez: “We will keep this offer firm for as long as we consider. But we will not be at the expense of what Atlético de Madrid can tell us on this issue”. “They know what our will is, if they want to do it, we will be delighted”. “At the moment, Atlético says they do NOT sell Álvarez. Now, they don’t sell him because seems they had no alternative...”. “I hope that if Atlético Madrid can find an alternative, they will consider our bid”.
2072312938866655441.raw_text:
🚨🔵🔴 Laporta on Julián Álvarez: “We will keep this offer firm for as long as we consider. But we will not be at the expense of what Atlético de Madrid can tell us on this issue”.

“They know what our will is, if they want to do it, we will be delighted”.

“At the moment, Atlético says they do NOT sell Álvarez. Now, they don’t sell him because seems they had no alternative...”.

“I hope that if Atlético Madrid can find an alternative, they will consider our bid”.

2072312623761170914.headline: Miguel Angel Gil
2072312623761170914.body: 🚨 Barça president Laporta: “We made this offer for Julián with all respect to Atlético. I spoke to both Cerezo and Miguel Angel Gil”. “The offer was sent and Atlético told me they had NO plan to sell Álvarez because they had NO alternatige as striker”, he added. “I told them that, if they have alternatives, the bid is firm”.

2072318314550903147.headline: Marcus Rashford
2072318314550903147.body: Marcus Rashford will keep his place when England take on DR Congo in the World Cup round of 32 later today. The Manchester United forward started and impressed in the final group-stage game against Panama and remains in the starting XI chosen by Thomas Tuchel, preferred over Anthony Gordon. Noni Madueke also starts with Bukayo Saka continuing to manage his fitness. Arsenal team-mate Declan Rice returns to the side after sitting out Saturday's match. Morgan Rogers started that game but drops back down to the bench in Atlanta. Djed Spence will start at right-back with Jarell Quansah and Reece James already having been ruled out with injury. ✍️ David_Ornstein 🔗 nyti.ms/4wj0iEH
2072318314550903147.raw_text:
Marcus Rashford will keep his place when England take on DR Congo in the World Cup round of 32 later today.

The Manchester United forward started and impressed in the final group-stage game against Panama and remains in the starting XI chosen by Thomas Tuchel, preferred over Anthony Gordon.

Noni Madueke also starts with Bukayo Saka continuing to manage his fitness. Arsenal team-mate Declan Rice returns to the side after sitting out Saturday's match.

Morgan Rogers started that game but drops back down to the bench in Atlanta.

Djed Spence will start at right-back with Jarell Quansah and Reece James already having been ruled out with injury.

✍️ @David_Ornstein 

🔗 nyti.ms/4wj0iEH

2072327238863782374.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo
2072327238863782374.body: Cristiano Ronaldo is yet to miss a minute of action at the World Cup and the notion of dropping one of the all-time greats is unthinkable, and - irrespective of the fact he intends to leave his role at the end of the tournament - unlikely to be a battle Roberto Martinez wants to take on. But, other than when Portugal are in the attacking third, they are playing with nine outfield players. Ronaldo does not track back, nor is he going to start doing that at this stage of his career. So, the onus is on those around him to do the running instead. And this is something of which Nani has first-hand experience at a major tournament having been asked to do it ahead of their Euro 2016 triumph by Fernando Santos, then Portugal’s head coach. “We need to understand that Brazil will do it (run) for Neymar,” he adds. “France will do for (Kylian) Mbappe, Argentina will do it for (Lionel) Messi. There is no discussion in these teams because and don’t have people so famous like Ronaldo. “Messi is always walking because he saves energy when the ball comes to him. He’s explosive and at his best in the the final third. “Ronaldo is the same in that it’s almost like: ‘Don’t waste your energy sprinting 60 yards.’” Read Nani’s exclusive interview with Dan_Sheldon_ ⤵️ 🔗 nyti.ms/4v9DdDx
2072327238863782374.raw_text:
Cristiano Ronaldo is yet to miss a minute of action at the World Cup and the notion of dropping one of the all-time greats is unthinkable, and - irrespective of the fact he intends to leave his role at the end of the tournament - unlikely to be a battle Roberto Martinez wants to take on.

But, other than when Portugal are in the attacking third, they are playing with nine outfield players. Ronaldo does not track back, nor is he going to start doing that at this stage of his career.

So, the onus is on those around him to do the running instead. And this is something of which Nani has first-hand experience at a major tournament having been asked to do it ahead of their Euro 2016 triumph by Fernando Santos, then Portugal’s head coach.

“We need to understand that Brazil will do it (run) for Neymar,” he adds. “France will do for (Kylian) Mbappe, Argentina will do it for (Lionel) Messi. There is no discussion in these teams because and don’t have people so famous like Ronaldo.

“Messi is always walking because he saves energy when the ball comes to him. He’s explosive and at his best in the the final third.

“Ronaldo is the same in that it’s almost like: ‘Don’t waste your energy sprinting 60 yards.’”

Read Nani’s exclusive interview with @Dan_Sheldon_ ⤵️

🔗 nyti.ms/4v9DdDx

2072333404289327325.headline: Declan Rice
2072333404289327325.body: Thomas Tuchel’s tweaks to England's line-up against DR Congo likely reflect a couple of things: 🔎 Declan Rice’s inclusion ahead of Morgan Rogers suggests he wants more protection against the counter-attacking threat of DR Congo, who will look to break from their 5-3-2 block 🔎 Djed Spence’s recovery pace at right-back should help, too, as Tuchel moves away from the six-attacker team he picked to try to break down Panama 🔎 Expect England to attack with five players across the front line, with left-back Nico O’Reilly stepping into midfield. 🔎 Marcus Rashford being preferred to Anthony Gordon on the left is partly because he has been the more effective of the two at this tournament, and partly because his inswinging crosses caused problems against Panama 📝 LiamTharmeCoach 🔗 nyti.ms/4vQKhpJ
2072333404289327325.raw_text:
Thomas Tuchel’s tweaks to England's line-up against DR Congo likely reflect a couple of things:

🔎 Declan Rice’s inclusion ahead of Morgan Rogers suggests he wants more protection against the counter-attacking threat of DR Congo, who will look to break from their 5-3-2 block

🔎 Djed Spence’s recovery pace at right-back should help, too, as Tuchel moves away from the six-attacker team he picked to try to break down Panama

🔎 Expect England to attack with five players across the front line, with left-back Nico O’Reilly stepping into midfield.

🔎 Marcus Rashford being preferred to Anthony Gordon on the left is partly because he has been the more effective of the two at this tournament, and partly because his inswinging crosses caused problems against Panama

📝 @LiamTharmeCoach 

🔗 nyti.ms/4vQKhpJ

2072366578083979359.headline: Vitor Pereira
2072366578083979359.body: 🚨 BREAKING: Vitor Pereira statement confirming his Nottingham Forest departure. Today marks the end of my journey as Head Coach of Nottingham Forest. I want to say a sincere thank you to everyone connected with this incredible football club. Although this decision came as a complete surprise to me and without any warning, I fully respect the club's right to make the decisions it believes are best for its future. Naturally, I am disappointed and saddened. I truly believed in what we were building together, and I leave with a sense of pride in everything we achieved over the past months. Together, we enjoyed a memorable end to the season. We secured the club's Premier League status, reached the semi-finals of the Europa League, and created moments that will stay with me forever. Most importantly, I saw a group of players grow in confidence, belief and togetherness. Watching them develop, fight for one another and represent this club with passion has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my career. I would like to thank my coaching staff, the players, everyone behind the scenes, and especially the supporters. From my first day, your passion, loyalty and unwavering support made me feel incredibly welcome. You were with us through every challenge and every success, and I will always be grateful for that. I leave Nottingham Forest with no bitterness or resentment—only respect, gratitude and wonderful memories. Football is full of unexpected moments, and while this chapter has ended sooner than I expected, I will always look back on my time here with pride and affection. I wish the players, the staff, the supporters and everyone at Nottingham Forest every success in the future. This is a special club, and I sincerely hope it continues to grow and achieve great things.
2072366578083979359.raw_text:
🚨 BREAKING: Vitor Pereira statement confirming his Nottingham Forest departure.

"Today marks the end of my journey as Head Coach of Nottingham Forest.

"I want to say a sincere thank you to everyone connected with this incredible football club. Although this decision came as a complete surprise to me and without any warning, I fully respect the club's right to make the decisions it believes are best for its future.

"Naturally, I am disappointed and saddened. I truly believed in what we were building together, and I leave with a sense of pride in everything we achieved over the past months.

"Together, we enjoyed a memorable end to the season. We secured the club's Premier League status, reached the semi-finals of the Europa League, and created moments that will stay with me forever. Most importantly, I saw a group of players grow in confidence, belief and togetherness. Watching them develop, fight for one another and represent this club with passion has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my career.

"I would like to thank my coaching staff, the players, everyone behind the scenes, and especially the supporters. From my first day, your passion, loyalty and unwavering support made me feel incredibly welcome. You were with us through every challenge and every success, and I will always be grateful for that.

"I leave Nottingham Forest with no bitterness or resentment—only respect, gratitude and wonderful memories. Football is full of unexpected moments, and while this chapter has ended sooner than I expected, I will always look back on my time here with pride and affection.

"I wish the players, the staff, the supporters and everyone at Nottingham Forest every success in the future. This is a special club, and I sincerely hope it continues to grow and achieve great things."

2072368904769609861.headline: Harry Kane
2072368904769609861.body: The verdict on Harry Kane's penalty claim from former Premier League referee Graham Scott (refsplaining): Harry Kane only has himself to blame: by dragging his left foot into the onrushing goalkeeper, he created doubt in the referee’s mind, and his penalty claim was waved away. On first viewing, England fans – including me – were no doubt screaming ‘penalty’ at their TV screens, and many referees would have agreed. There is a good case for one, as goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi undoubtedly makes significant contact with Kane, and there is no way the VAR would have recommended a review had Jordanian referee Adham Makhadmeh pointed to the spot. However, Kane goes to great lengths to ensure that contact occurs, plants both feet on the ground and falls theatrically – all clues to the referee that the England striker was having him on. Fair play to Makhadmeh for standing strong. Had Kane carried on running as normal, he may have been brought down anyway – and earned the penalty he craved – or he might have reached the ball and rolled it into the empty net. I wish he had. England vs DR Congo live blog: bit.ly/4vS1z5G
2072368904769609861.raw_text:
The verdict on Harry Kane's penalty claim from former Premier League referee Graham Scott (@refsplaining):

"Harry Kane only has himself to blame: by dragging his left foot into the onrushing goalkeeper, he created doubt in the referee’s mind, and his penalty claim was waved away.

"On first viewing, England fans – including me – were no doubt screaming ‘penalty’ at their TV screens, and many referees would have agreed.

"There is a good case for one, as goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi undoubtedly makes significant contact with Kane, and there is no way the VAR would have recommended a review had Jordanian referee Adham Makhadmeh pointed to the spot.

"However, Kane goes to great lengths to ensure that contact occurs, plants both feet on the ground and falls theatrically – all clues to the referee that the England striker was having him on. Fair play to Makhadmeh for standing strong.

"Had Kane carried on running as normal, he may have been brought down anyway – and earned the penalty he craved – or he might have reached the ball and rolled it into the empty net. I wish he had."

England vs DR Congo live blog: bit.ly/4vS1z5G

2072461422202671553.headline: The Belgium
2072461422202671553.body: With 86 minutes on the clock it was effectively all over. Senegal were 2-0 up, their fans were partying in the stands and Belgium were going through the motions. The Belgium golden generation obituaries were being written, not least after their greatest ever player Kevin De Bruyne – plus a seemingly disgusted Jeremy Doku – were substituted after just 55 minutes. Senegal were excellent value for their famous imminent victory. Precisely 159 seconds later, the scoreline was 2-2 thanks to one of the most unlikely World Cup comebacks in living memory. It was a comeback as devastatingly clinical as it was unlikely, recalling a previous miraculous Belgium recovery when they came from 2-0 down to beat Japan in the last 16 in 2018. This was the first occasion this tournament of a team recovering from 2-0 down and was an occasion no one inside Seattle Stadium will ever forget. 📝 TimSpiers bit.ly/4vcuVuy
2072461422202671553.raw_text:
With 86 minutes on the clock it was effectively all over. Senegal were 2-0 up, their fans were partying in the stands and Belgium were going through the motions.

The Belgium golden generation obituaries were being written, not least after their greatest ever player Kevin De Bruyne – plus a seemingly disgusted Jeremy Doku – were substituted after just 55 minutes. Senegal were excellent value for their famous imminent victory.

Precisely 159 seconds later, the scoreline was 2-2 thanks to one of the most unlikely World Cup comebacks in living memory.

It was a comeback as devastatingly clinical as it was unlikely, recalling a previous miraculous Belgium recovery when they came from 2-0 down to beat Japan in the last 16 in 2018. 

This was the first occasion this tournament of a team recovering from 2-0 down and was an occasion no one inside Seattle Stadium will ever forget.

📝 @TimSpiers 

bit.ly/4vcuVuy

2072502081198665876.headline: Folarin Balogun
2072502081198665876.body: 🚨 USMNT through to a R16 tie in Seattle after a 2-0 victory over Bosnia. Folarin Balogun with the opener before Malik Tillman scored a fantastic free-kick. Balogun was sent off before the USMNT doubled their lead following a VAR check after catching the back of Tarik Muharemović's leg. He'll miss the game against Belgium, and the red will no doubt anger Mauricio Pochettino. USMNT showed tremendous character and quality when it mattered. First knockout stage win at a World Cup in 24 years.🇺🇸🇧🇦

2072515439071039669.headline: The Athletic
2072515439071039669.body: 𝗖𝗮𝗻 𝗙𝗼𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗻 𝗕𝗮𝗹𝗼𝗴𝘂𝗻'𝘀 𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗱 𝗯𝗲 𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗲𝗱? A team cannot appeal against a red card or the subsequent suspension, per a FIFA official. When asked by The Athletic whether a red card can be appealed a FIFA official pointed to its regulations and Article 66.4 which states: “A sending-off automatically incurs suspension from the subsequent match. The FIFA judicial bodies may impose additional match suspensions and other disciplinary measures.” FIFA’s Disciplinary Committee also has the power to extend any ban. More details — free to read: bit.ly/3RejN2k

“A sending-off automatically incurs suspension from the subsequent match. The FIFA judicial bodies may impose additional match suspensions and other disciplinary measures.”

FIFA’s Disciplinary Committee also has the power to extend any ban.

2072565506692018262.headline: Disciplinary Code
2072565506692018262.body: Despite the community note here, it remains the case that FIFA's regulations do NOT allow an appeal. Article 9, Section 6: No protests may be made about the referee’s decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code. Article 10, Section 6: If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card [second caution], they will automatically be suspended from their team’s subsequent match. In addition, further sanctions may be imposed. Article 66.4: A sending-off automatically incurs suspension from the subsequent match. The FIFA judicial bodies may impose additional match suspensions and other disciplinary measures.
2072565506692018262.raw_text:
Despite the community note here, it remains the case that FIFA's regulations do NOT allow an appeal.

Article 9, Section 6: "No protests may be made about the referee’s decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code."

Article 10, Section 6: "If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card [second caution], they will automatically be suspended from their team’s subsequent match. In addition, further sanctions may be imposed."

Article 66.4: "A sending-off automatically incurs suspension from the subsequent match. The FIFA judicial bodies may impose additional match suspensions and other disciplinary measures."

2072567371630342603.headline: Mauricio Pochettino
2072567371630342603.body: U.S. national team head coach Mauricio Pochettino believes Folarin Balogun should never have been sent off after the striker’s controversial dismissal threatened to derail their World Cup victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina. “For me? Never a red card,” Pochettino said. “Never an intention to step on the player. It was a normal action in football that happened by accident.” Balogun was sent off in the second half after a video review showed that he’d stomped on the ankle of Bosnia’s Tarik Muharemovic while challenging for a ball. “He’s very disappointed,” Pochettino said of Balogun. “It was an action that wasn’t intention, he’s sad. … We cannot do anything to change this feeling. This is soccer, this is football. For sure he’s going to help us to perform and hopefully we can go to the next round so he can be available again.” More from tombogert 🔗 nyti.ms/4gLVTFO
2072567371630342603.raw_text:
U.S. national team head coach Mauricio Pochettino believes Folarin Balogun should never have been sent off after the striker’s controversial dismissal threatened to derail their World Cup victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina.

“For me? Never a red card,” Pochettino said. “Never an intention to step on the player. It was a normal action in football that happened by accident.”

Balogun was sent off in the second half after a video review showed that he’d stomped on the ankle of Bosnia’s Tarik Muharemovic while challenging for a ball.

“He’s very disappointed,” Pochettino said of Balogun. 

“It was an action that wasn’t intention, he’s sad. … We cannot do anything to change this feeling. This is soccer, this is football. For sure he’s going to help us to perform and hopefully we can go to the next round so he can be available again.”

More from @tombogert 

🔗 nyti.ms/4gLVTFO

2072600228708958272.body: Roberto De Zerbi can come across spiky. But, in football terms, he brings gentrification. When he moves into your club, nice things tend to follow. This remains one of the principal reasons to hire him. Making signings for a club, providing they’re ones De Zerbi approves of, becomes significantly easier. If you get a player in front of De Zerbi, all that’s left to do for the clubs is to agree a fee. He has a pull that few coaches possess. 2072606414896332819.headline: Luis Milla
2072606414896332819.body: Como 1907 is delighted to announce the signing of Spanish midfielder Luis Milla from Getafe CF. The 31-year-old joins the club on a permanent transfer until June 2029, bringing experience, creativity and technical quality to Cesc Fàbregas’ midfield. Milla joined Getafe in 2022 and quickly became one of the club’s most influential players. Renowned for his composure in possession, vision and ability to control the tempo of a game, he enjoyed an outstanding 2025/26 campaign, recording ten assists in 37 league appearances to finish as La Liga’s second-highest assist provider. Cesc Fàbregas, Head Coach of Como 1907, commented: “Luis is a player with great understanding of the game. He gives us control, quality and experience in midfield, but above all he has the personality and ambition we look for. He’ll help the team in many ways, both with and without the ball, and we’re delighted to welcome him to Como.” Read the full statement on comofootball.com
2072606414896332819.raw_text:
Como 1907 is delighted to announce the signing of Spanish midfielder Luis Milla from Getafe CF.

The 31-year-old joins the club on a permanent transfer until June 2029, bringing experience, creativity and technical quality to Cesc Fàbregas’ midfield.

Milla joined Getafe in 2022 and quickly became one of the club’s most influential players. Renowned for his composure in possession, vision and ability to control the tempo of a game, he enjoyed an outstanding 2025/26 campaign, recording ten assists in 37 league appearances to finish as La Liga’s second-highest assist provider.

Cesc Fàbregas, Head Coach of Como 1907, commented: “Luis is a player with great understanding of the game. He gives us control, quality and experience in midfield, but above all he has the personality and ambition we look for. He’ll help the team in many ways, both with and without the ball, and we’re delighted to welcome him to Como.”

Read the full statement on comofootball.com

2072606248088846700.headline: Pako Ayestaran
2072606248088846700.body: Aston Villa can confirm a small number of personnel changes to Unai Emery's coaching staff. Pako Ayestaran (Assistant Coach) and Antonio Rodríguez Saravia (Individual Performance Coach) will both depart the club, having been part of Unai's coaching staff since his appointment in 2022 and have played an integral role in the club's recent on-field success. José María Sanz will join as Assistant Coach, subject to visa approval. Sanz is a well-respected coach with over 20 years of experience with the likes of Valencia, Sheffield United and Osasuna. Both Pako and Rodri will always remain hugely popular at Aston Villa and everyone at the club would like to thank them for their service as well as wishing them all the very best for the future.
2072606248088846700.raw_text:
Aston Villa can confirm a small number of personnel changes to Unai Emery's coaching staff.

Pako Ayestaran (Assistant Coach) and Antonio Rodríguez Saravia (Individual Performance Coach) will both depart the club, having been part of Unai's coaching staff since his appointment in 2022 and have played an integral role in the club's recent on-field success.

José María Sanz will join as Assistant Coach, subject to visa approval. Sanz is a well-respected coach with over 20 years of experience with the likes of Valencia, Sheffield United and Osasuna.

Both Pako and Rodri will always remain hugely popular at Aston Villa and everyone at the club would like to thank them for their service as well as wishing them all the very best for the future.

2072635391824240774.headline: Mateus Fernandes
2072635391824240774.body: Tottenham Hotspur have completed the signing of West Ham United midfielder Mateus Fernandes. The Athletic | Football (TheAthleticFC) Tottenham Hotspur have won the race for West Ham United for midfielder Mateus Fernandes. Spurs have made the highest offer to West Ham — believed to be worth a £85million (€98m, $112m) guaranteed fee according to sources — and the 21-year-old has decided to join them. The Portugal international has attracted interest from multiple clubs, including Manchester United, but is now set to move to north London after West Ham stood firm on their valuation. For United, the transfer fee and the player’s salary went higher than they were willing to pay, and they will now shift their focus to other targets. More from David_Ornstein 🔗 nyti.ms/4gLVR0z —
2072635391824240774.raw_text:
Tottenham Hotspur have completed the signing of West Ham United midfielder Mateus Fernandes.




The Athletic | Football (@TheAthleticFC)

Tottenham Hotspur have won the race for West Ham United for midfielder Mateus Fernandes.

Spurs have made the highest offer to West Ham — believed to be worth a £85million (€98m, $112m) guaranteed fee according to sources — and the 21-year-old has decided to join them.

The Portugal international has attracted interest from multiple clubs, including Manchester United, but is now set to move to north London after West Ham stood firm on their valuation.

For United, the transfer fee and the player’s salary went higher than they were willing to pay, and they will now shift their focus to other targets.

More from @David_Ornstein 

🔗 nyti.ms/4gLVR0z





— https://nitter.net/TheAthleticFC/status/2071995180924735641#m

2072634848708046876.headline: Alvaro Rodriguez
2072634848708046876.body: Bournemouth are in advanced talks to sign Elche forward Alvaro Rodriguez. The deal, if completed, will be worth €30million (£25.7m) and the 21-year-old Real Madrid academy graduate is nearing a five-year deal. The deal consists of a €25m fee with €5m in add-ons and will keep him at the Vitality Stadium until 2031. Madrid are set to receive half of the fee (€12.5m; £10.6m) and lose their rights over the Uruguay youth international, who left the Santiago Bernabeu last summer. More from MarioCortegana and JessMCHopkins 🔗 nyti.ms/4eWLFjs
2072634848708046876.raw_text:
Bournemouth are in advanced talks to sign Elche forward Alvaro Rodriguez.

The deal, if completed, will be worth €30million (£25.7m) and the 21-year-old Real Madrid academy graduate is nearing a five-year deal. 

The deal consists of a €25m fee with €5m in add-ons and will keep him at the Vitality Stadium until 2031.

Madrid are set to receive half of the fee (€12.5m; £10.6m) and lose their rights over the Uruguay youth international, who left the Santiago Bernabeu last summer.

2072669663570309432.headline: Elliot Anderson
2072669663570309432.body: Manchester City have reached an agreement to complete the signing of midfielder Elliot Anderson from Nottingham Forest in a deal worth £116million. Anderson, 23, has signed a five-year contract at the Etihad Stadium which, if all bonuses are realised, will be worth in the region of £300,000 per week. The deal also includes the option to extend by a further 12 months. ✍️ LeonImber 🔗 nyti.ms/44sl4pL The Athletic | Football (TheAthleticFC) Manchester City have agreed a £116m ($153m) deal to sign Elliot Anderson from Nottingham Forest. The fee — a fixed figure with no add-ons — is the most ever paid for a British player and represents a new record for City, eclipsing the £100m they paid Aston Villa for Jack Grealish in 2021. Forest had previously been looking for the fixed fee of any sale to be higher than the £125m ($165m) British record Liverpool paid Newcastle United for Alexander Isak last summer. Full story from David_Ornstein ⬇️ bit.ly/4w5sw5D —
2072669663570309432.raw_text:
Manchester City have reached an agreement to complete the signing of midfielder Elliot Anderson from Nottingham Forest in a deal worth £116million.

Anderson, 23, has signed a five-year contract at the Etihad Stadium which, if all bonuses are realised, will be worth in the region of £300,000 per week.

The deal also includes the option to extend by a further 12 months.

✍️ @LeonImber 

🔗 nyti.ms/44sl4pL




The Athletic | Football (@TheAthleticFC)

Manchester City have agreed a £116m ($153m) deal to sign Elliot Anderson from Nottingham Forest.

The fee — a fixed figure with no add-ons — is the most ever paid for a British player and represents a new record for City, eclipsing the £100m they paid Aston Villa for Jack Grealish in 2021.

Forest had previously been looking for the fixed fee of any sale to be higher than the £125m ($165m) British record Liverpool paid Newcastle United for Alexander Isak last summer.

Full story from @David_Ornstein ⬇️

bit.ly/4w5sw5D

2072689626754527556.headline: Luis Suarez
2072689626754527556.body: Sixteen years ago today, Luis Suarez became public enemy No 1 in Ghana. With Uruguay and Ghana locked at 1-1 at the very end of extra time in the World Cup quarter-final, the former Liverpool and Barcelona forward palmed away Dominic Adiyah's goalbound effort on the line. He was duly shown a red card. Suarez appeared to be in floods of tears as he headed down the tunnel, but his mood was instantly transformed as Asamoah Gyan's penalty struck the crossbar. Uruguay went on to win 4-2 on penalties to progress to the 2010 World Cup semi-finals, where they lost 3-2 to the Netherlands.
2072689626754527556.raw_text:
Sixteen years ago today, Luis Suarez became public enemy No 1 in Ghana.

With Uruguay and Ghana locked at 1-1 at the very end of extra time in the World Cup quarter-final, the former Liverpool and Barcelona forward palmed away Dominic Adiyah's goalbound effort on the line.

He was duly shown a red card.

Suarez appeared to be in floods of tears as he headed down the tunnel, but his mood was instantly transformed as Asamoah Gyan's penalty struck the crossbar.

Uruguay went on to win 4-2 on penalties to progress to the 2010 World Cup semi-finals, where they lost 3-2 to the Netherlands.

2072727354586042734.headline: Vítor Pereira
2072727354586042734.body: Nottingham Forest can confirm that Vítor Pereira has left his role as Head Coach following the Club’s decision to exercise a mutual break clause in his contract. His coaching staff – Filipe Almeida, Luis Miguel, Bruno Moura, Marco Knoop and Pedro Lopes – have also left the Club. We would like to place on record our sincere thanks to Vítor and his team for their hard work and commitment throughout their time at the Club. They represented Forest with enormous dedication, helping to secure the Club’s status in the Premier League, guiding the squad to the semi-finals of the UEFA Europa League, and providing us with the foundations upon which we will continue to build. Everyone at Nottingham Forest is grateful for their considerable efforts and for the relationships they forged with our players, staff and supporters. We thank them all for their service and wish them every success in the next stage of their careers.
2072727354586042734.raw_text:
Nottingham Forest can confirm that Vítor Pereira has left his role as Head Coach following the Club’s decision to exercise a mutual break clause in his contract.

His coaching staff – Filipe Almeida, Luis Miguel, Bruno Moura, Marco Knoop and Pedro Lopes – have also left the Club.

We would like to place on record our sincere thanks to Vítor and his team for their hard work and commitment throughout their time at the Club. 

They represented Forest with enormous dedication, helping to secure the Club’s status in the Premier League, guiding the squad to the semi-finals of the UEFA Europa League, and providing us with the foundations upon which we will continue to build.

Everyone at Nottingham Forest is grateful for their considerable efforts and for the relationships they forged with our players, staff and supporters.  

We thank them all for their service and wish them every success in the next stage of their careers.

2072952124267544743.headline: Real Madrid
2072952124267544743.body: 🚨 Real Madrid issue a statement saying they have no intention of signing Enzo Fernandez. In light of the reports and statements that have appeared in recent days regarding an alleged interest from Real Madrid CF in the player Enzo Fernández, the club wishes to state that it has not made any effort, either direct or indirect, to sign the aforementioned player and, likewise, has no intention of undertaking such an operation. Real Madrid wishes to express its utmost respect for Enzo Fernández, a great footballer whose career and quality are widely known, as well as for Chelsea FC, a club with which it maintains an excellent institutional relationship. Precisely because of the respect that an entity like Chelsea FC deserves and because of the principles of institutional loyalty that have always governed the actions of Real Madrid, the club considers it necessary to categorically deny speculations that are unfounded and do not correspond to reality. Real Madrid regrets that, despite the clarity of the facts and the lack of any action on the part of the club, information continues to be disseminated that does not correspond to reality and that only contributes to generating confusion among fans and unnecessarily harming the entities and people involved.
2072952124267544743.raw_text:
🚨 Real Madrid issue a statement saying they have "no intention" of signing Enzo Fernandez.

"In light of the reports and statements that have appeared in recent days regarding an alleged interest from Real Madrid CF in the player Enzo Fernández, the club wishes to state that it has not made any effort, either direct or indirect, to sign the aforementioned player and, likewise, has no intention of undertaking such an operation.

"Real Madrid wishes to express its utmost respect for Enzo Fernández, a great footballer whose career and quality are widely known, as well as for Chelsea FC, a club with which it maintains an excellent institutional relationship.

"Precisely because of the respect that an entity like Chelsea FC deserves and because of the principles of institutional loyalty that have always governed the actions of Real Madrid, the club considers it necessary to categorically deny speculations that are unfounded and do not correspond to reality.

"Real Madrid regrets that, despite the clarity of the facts and the lack of any action on the part of the club, information continues to be disseminated that does not correspond to reality and that only contributes to generating confusion among fans and unnecessarily harming the entities and people involved."

Good luck to everyone taking part, and we hope you all enjoy the day."
Tyrrell Hatton & Modest Golf

Nagelsmann will also be joined in his departure by assistants Benjamin Glück and Benjamin Hübner. 2072991616571244852.headline: World Cup
2072991616571244852.body: Going through my mom's passing, we had one main goal as brothers and we knew it was to take care of our dad. To lose a mum, it's not easy for anybody. But knowing that dad had lost his partner, his day one, we just had a common goal to make sure dad was taken care of. Then, we lost dad the next year. When dad passed away, Stephen was the one who called me and gave me the news. He said: OK, it's just us two now. My mom was just so... mom. She was a big talker, she showed her emotions. She would have been jumping around with joy. She would have the world's biggest smile on her face. My dad was more of the silent type. But dad, knowing the sport, knowing what this means for the country, knowing how big the World Cup is, his heart would be melting. He would have no words. But you would see his emotion on his face. We owe them everything. Mauro Eustaquio tells of his childhood with brother and Canada hero Stephen, the love of their parents, and the loss they feel. 🔗 nyti.ms/4fcUAyta
2072991616571244852.raw_text:
Going through my mom's passing, we had one main goal as brothers and we knew it was to take care of our dad. To lose a mum, it's not easy for anybody. But knowing that dad had lost his partner, his day one, we just had a common goal to make sure dad was taken care of.

Then, we lost dad the next year. When dad passed away, Stephen was the one who called me and gave me the news. He said: "OK, it's just us two now."

My mom was just so... mom. She was a big talker, she showed her emotions. She would have been jumping around with joy. She would have the world's biggest smile on her face.

My dad was more of the silent type. But dad, knowing the sport, knowing what this means for the country, knowing how big the World Cup is, his heart would be melting. He would have no words. But you would see his emotion on his face. We owe them everything.

Mauro Eustaquio tells of his childhood with brother and Canada hero Stephen, the love of their parents, and the loss they feel.

🔗 nyti.ms/4fcUAyta

2073025484623860184.headline: Bayern Munich
2073025484623860184.body: Bayern Munich have completed the signing of defender Nathaniel Brown from Eintracht Frankfurt The Athletic reported in June that Brown was committed to joining Bayern, having also attracted interest from Arsenal. He plays primarily as a left-back, but can also play further forward, as well as, if need be, on the right side of defence. His technical ability on the ball has been a great asset for Julian Nagelsmann’s national side, for whom he is currently first choice, and will likely prove a similar virtue for Bayern, whose possession-based style and positional fluidity he should easily adapt to. The next question is what this means for Alphonso Davies, the Canadian international full-back. Davies is a first-choice when fit and has had a successful Bayern career, but he has suffered a succession of injuries over recent seasons and has become difficult to depend upon. More from SebSB 🔗 bit.ly/4fhpuWp
2073025484623860184.raw_text:
Bayern Munich have completed the signing of defender Nathaniel Brown from Eintracht Frankfurt

The Athletic reported in June that Brown was committed to joining Bayern, having also attracted interest from Arsenal.

He plays primarily as a left-back, but can also play further forward, as well as, if need be, on the right side of defence.

His technical ability on the ball has been a great asset for Julian Nagelsmann’s national side, for whom he is currently first choice, and will likely prove a similar virtue for Bayern, whose possession-based style and positional fluidity he should easily adapt to.

The next question is what this means for Alphonso Davies, the Canadian international full-back. Davies is a first-choice when fit and has had a successful Bayern career, but he has suffered a succession of injuries over recent seasons and has become difficult to depend upon.

More from @SebSB

🔗 bit.ly/4fhpuWp

2073099451615326658.headline: Evangelos Marinakis
2073099451615326658.body: Evangelos Marinakis wanted a deal for Elliot Anderson worth £126m, with an initial £106m being topped up by £20m in easily achievable bonuses. These included playing a certain number of games and winning trophies that Forest considered realistic. Manchester City preferred add-ons that included winning the Champions League, which Forest refused as they did not feel it was an especially realistic prospect. When Declan Rice joined Arsenal, one element of the add-ons related to Champions League qualification, rather than winning the competition, and this was part of Forest’s thinking. SamLee and lauriewhitwell tell the inside story of Anderson’s British record move ⤵️ 🔗 nyti.ms/4b7yUkR
2073099451615326658.raw_text:
Evangelos Marinakis wanted a deal for Elliot Anderson worth £126m, with an initial £106m being topped up by £20m in easily achievable bonuses. These included playing a certain number of games and winning trophies that Forest considered realistic.

Manchester City preferred add-ons that included winning the Champions League, which Forest refused as they did not feel it was an especially realistic prospect.

When Declan Rice joined Arsenal, one element of the add-ons related to Champions League qualification, rather than winning the competition, and this was part of Forest’s thinking.

@SamLee and @lauriewhitwell tell the inside story of Anderson’s British record move ⤵️

🔗 nyti.ms/4b7yUkR

2073090077949911041.headline: Nestory Irankunda
2073090077949911041.body: Control. Hit. Goal. Cartwheel. Backflip. Get used to watching Nestory Irankunda. He broke out aged 15 at Adelaide United in Australia - his first goal was a direct free kick. As a teen sensation, he netted 16 times across 55 A-League appearances for Adelaide - a club-record sale to Bayern Munich followed before moving to Watford in England’s second tier last summer, where his debut season was promising. His achievements are all the more remarkable considering he had been born in a refugee camp in Tanzania in 2006 to Burundian parents, who had fled because of the civil war, and moved to Australia in his infancy. Irankunda announced himself on the world stage with Australia’s first goal of the 2026 World Cup against Turkey. Now, against Egypt, he now has the chance to help his country win a men’s World Cup knockout game for the first time. ✍️ LiamTharmeCoach 🔗 nyti.ms/44aHDiy
2073090077949911041.raw_text:
Control. Hit. Goal. Cartwheel. Backflip. Get used to watching Nestory Irankunda.

He broke out aged 15 at Adelaide United in Australia - his first goal was a direct free kick.

As a teen sensation, he netted 16 times across 55 A-League appearances for Adelaide - a club-record sale to Bayern Munich followed before moving to Watford in England’s second tier last summer, where his debut season was promising.

His achievements are all the more remarkable considering he had been born in a refugee camp in Tanzania in 2006 to Burundian parents, who had fled because of the civil war, and moved to Australia in his infancy.

Irankunda announced himself on the world stage with Australia’s first goal of the 2026 World Cup against Turkey. Now, against Egypt, he now has the chance to help his country win a men’s World Cup knockout game for the first time.

✍️ @LiamTharmeCoach 

🔗 nyti.ms/44aHDiy

2073086768392360053.headline: Evangelos Marinakis
2073086768392360053.body: Sources at Nottingham Forest say Evangelos Marinakis was always open to selling Elliot Anderson this summer, but the owner was determined to get a top price. With the deal with Manchester City still to be concluded, Marinakis gave the impression in mid-June that he would be prepared to sell Anderson to another club for a lower price. Manchester United enquired and even discussed the prospect of making Anderson captain at some point in the future. But in the end, United concluded the transfer fee involved was more than they wanted to pay. SamLee and lauriewhitwell tell the inside story of Anderson’s British record move ⤵️ 🔗 nyti.ms/4b7yUkR
2073086768392360053.raw_text:
Sources at Nottingham Forest say Evangelos Marinakis was always open to selling Elliot Anderson this summer, but the owner was determined to get a top price.

With the deal with Manchester City still to be concluded, Marinakis gave the impression in mid-June that he would be prepared to sell Anderson to another club for a lower price.

Manchester United enquired and even discussed the prospect of making Anderson captain at some point in the future.

But in the end, United concluded the transfer fee involved was more than they wanted to pay.

@SamLee and @lauriewhitwell tell the inside story of Anderson’s British record move ⤵️

🔗 nyti.ms/4b7yUkR

2073084505309774030.raw_text:

2073044094503305492.headline: Us And Our People
2073044094503305492.body: Us And Our People. 💙 The club has worked with Suffolk FA to offer all 115 registered grassroots youth football clubs in the county a grant to cover the cost of a full kit for one of their teams for the 2026/27 season. At the time of launch, more than 70 have accepted this offer with the process still open and more expected to follow. 👕 This represents a significant commitment from the club in support of the rich grassroots football scene in the county, and embodies the meaning behind the ‘Us And Our People’ mantra, which has become important to the club and signifies the connection between the players on the pitch and the community of Ipswich and Suffolk. 🏡 Video
2073044094503305492.raw_text:
Us And Our People. 💙

The club has worked with Suffolk FA to offer all 115 registered grassroots youth football clubs in the county a grant to cover the cost of a full kit for one of their teams for the 2026/27 season. At the time of launch, more than 70 have accepted this offer with the process still open and more expected to follow. 👕

This represents a significant commitment from the club in support of the rich grassroots football scene in the county, and embodies the meaning behind the ‘Us And Our People’ mantra, which has become important to the club and signifies the connection between the players on the pitch and the community of Ipswich and Suffolk. 🏡

2073156835591483685.headline: Red Bull
2073156835591483685.body: 🚨💣 BREAKING | Jürgen Klopp confirms our news: He wants the Germany job – but it will take time. Speaking to MagentaTV, Klopp said: “Yes, I can confirm the talks. Things moved pretty quickly. Julian stepped down. The DFB are looking for a successor. And they’re talking to me.” On what still needs to happen: “Time. I’m under contract with Red Bull. I’ve said I’m interested in the talks. They will be intensive because this isn’t only about Julian Nagelsmann.” “I also have to speak with Oliver Mintzlaff. He’s my employer. We’ve already touched on a few things. I assume he won’t stand in the way. I’ve been there for 19 months. It was an intense time.” ⚠️ “I’m ready. Once the talks begin, your mind starts racing. We have to change things fundamentally.” SkySportDE 🇩🇪
2073156835591483685.raw_text:
🚨💣 BREAKING | Jürgen Klopp confirms our news: He wants the Germany job – but it will take time.

Speaking to @MagentaTV, Klopp said: “Yes, I can confirm the talks. Things moved pretty quickly. Julian stepped down. The DFB are looking for a successor. And they’re talking to me.”

On what still needs to happen: “Time. I’m under contract with Red Bull. I’ve said I’m interested in the talks. They will be intensive because this isn’t only about Julian Nagelsmann.”

“I also have to speak with Oliver Mintzlaff. He’s my employer. We’ve already touched on a few things. I assume he won’t stand in the way. I’ve been there for 19 months. It was an intense time.”

⚠️ “I’m ready. Once the talks begin, your mind starts racing. We have to change things fundamentally.” 

@SkySportDE 🇩🇪

2073145227263824308.headline: Oliver Mintzlaff
2073145227263824308.body: 🚨⏳ The DFB are now working on reaching an agreement with Jürgen Klopp. He is the federation’s top candidate and wants to become Germany head coach, something that was never in doubt. Oliver Mintzlaff and Red Bull are not expected to be an obstacle, but key details and parameters still need to be discussed. No agreements have been reached yet. Talks are still at an early stage. But tendency is clear: Klopp ist set to become the new Bundestrainer. SkySportDE 🇩🇪

2073346328705327462.headline: Paul Gascoigne
2073346328705327462.body: On this day, 1990... Paul Gascoigne's tears in Turin are arguably the most iconic image in English footballing history. Already on a booking after a foul on Belgium's Enzo Scifo in the last 16, the charismatic midfielder lunged at Thomas Berthold in the 98th minute of England's semi-final against West Germany. A second caution meant that the 23-year-old, England's best player in the tournament, would miss the final should his team get there. The challenge prompted Gary Lineker to say to manager Sir Bobby Robson 'have a word with him' as England's youngest player couldn't hold back the tears. England would go on to suffer penalty heartbreak and despite becoming a national hero, 'Gazza' would never get a shot at the World Cup again.
2073346328705327462.raw_text:
On this day, 1990...

Paul Gascoigne's tears in Turin are arguably the most iconic image in English footballing history. 

Already on a booking after a foul on Belgium's Enzo Scifo in the last 16, the charismatic midfielder lunged at Thomas Berthold in the 98th minute of England's semi-final against West Germany.

A second caution meant that the 23-year-old, England's best player in the tournament, would miss the final should his team get there.

The challenge prompted Gary Lineker to say to manager Sir Bobby Robson 'have a word with him' as England's youngest player couldn't hold back the tears.

England would go on to suffer penalty heartbreak and despite becoming a national hero, 'Gazza' would never get a shot at the World Cup again.

2073719967996953034.headline: Bernd Neuendorf
2073719967996953034.body: 🚨🆕 A first phone call between DFB president Bernd Neuendorf and Red Bull regarding Jürgen Klopp took place yesterday. Further talks today. First direct meeting between a DFB delegation, Klopp and agent Marc Kosicke is planned in New York next week. Neuendorf and Hans-Joachim Watzke are expected to attend, with senior Red Bull representatives also a possibility. No agreement between the DFB and Red Bull over Klopp’s exit yet. Talks are ongoing and expected to continue for several more days. Red Bull shareholders are pushing for a compensation fee. As confirmed by Klopp himself: he wants to become Germany head coach. And he will. SkySportDE 🇩🇪

2073739041661493345.headline: Transfer update
2073739041661493345.body: Mexican supporters did gather for a ‘serenade’ near the England team hotel overnight. It sounds romantic. Much more romantic than a load of people gathering outside the team hotel in the dead of night, and making a racket in an attempt to prevent Mexico’s next opponents from sleeping. The scenes in Mexico City were on a much smaller scale than those ahead of the Ecuador game last week. This time, the police were prepared. There were two perimeters set up, one preventing vehicles from passing, the other stopping anyone on foot. Even the inner ring was 200 metres from England’s hotel. Crowds gathered, but the police eventually moved them on, as they did when a second wave - a group of fans played music and let off fireworks - later mustered on a footbridge. jacklang investigates ⤵️ 🔗 nytimes.com/athletic/7422742… Video
2073739041661493345.raw_text:
Mexican supporters did gather for a ‘serenade’ near the England team hotel overnight.

It sounds romantic.

Much more romantic than a load of people gathering outside the team hotel in the dead of night, and making a racket in an attempt to prevent Mexico’s next opponents from sleeping.

The scenes in Mexico City were on a much smaller scale than those ahead of the Ecuador game last week. This time, the police were prepared.

There were two perimeters set up, one preventing vehicles from passing, the other stopping anyone on foot. Even the inner ring was 200 metres from England’s hotel.

Crowds gathered, but the police eventually moved them on, as they did when a second wave - a group of fans played music and let off fireworks - later mustered on a footbridge.

@jacklang investigates ⤵️

🔗 nytimes.com/athletic/7422742…

Video

2073716266024456376.headline: World Cup
2073716266024456376.body: At this World Cup, several players such as England’s Djed Spence, Algeria’s Luca Zidane, Uruguay’s Sebastian Caceres and Austria’s Stefan Posch have been sporting protective facial gear. A mask is not a cure nor does it eliminate the risk of further injury or complications, but it is a compromise between recovery and competition. The mask-making process is a complex one, as doctors, surgeons and designers explain... ✍️ charlotteharpur 🔗 nyti.ms/4p3jsMp

deferred.headline: Michael Olise
deferred.body: Michael Olise is set to stay; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
🚨❌ FC Bayern have also received confirmation from Michael Olise’s camp that there have been no talks, meetings or negotiations with Real Madrid so far.
No offer from Real Madrid either. Bayern are not open to negotiations. Olise has not expressed any desire to leave.
Instead, Bayern are working behind the scenes on contract extension beyond 2029 and want to make him one of the club’s top earners.  🇫🇷

deferred.headline: Jones
deferred.body: Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.
deferred.text:
Inter made a fresh approach for Curtis Jones last week asking whether a deal was possible for around €25m.
This was a conversation not a formal or written offer.
Liverpool made it clear no sale at this price, especially given numbers other midfielders are expected to be sold for this summer.
Liverpool are prepared to keep Jones unless a suitable bid arises, even if that means losing him on a free next summer.
Liverpool have never had concerns about Jones' attitude or professionalism despite Inter reapproaching the club, and growing interest elsewhere.
Understand the club's position has hardened as a result of Inter's latest contact. If the right offer emerges, a sale will be sanctioned, but Inter would have to significantly increase their valuation to get Liverpool to engage.
Worth noting, Liverpool are also comfortable with Jones staying, and if that happens, and he impresses Andoni Iraola, they could yet decide to engage in new contract talks.🔴
deferred.draft_caption:
📝 REPORTED: Jones (Liverpool)
Jones is set to stay at Liverpool; no exit is planned at this stage.

#FPLVortex #Transfers #LFC #PremierLeague
